cards.title: NVIDIA Cosmos 3 Edge: 4B-Parameter On-Device World Model for Robotics
cards.why_it_matters: A compact open world-action model achieving real-time robot control at 15 Hz on Jetson hardware is directly relevant for anyone working on embodied AI or edge inference pipelines.
cards.summary: NVIDIA's Cosmos 3 Edge is a 4B-parameter open model combining autoregressive and diffusion transformer towers with shared multimodal attention, targeting edge deployment on Jetson, RTX, and DGX hardware. It runs at robot-control resolution (640×360), generates 32 actions per inference, achieves 15 Hz control on Jetson Thor, and ranks #1 on VANTAGE-Bench among 4B-scale models.

cards.title: Kimi K3: Moonshot AI's 2.8T MoE Flagship Challenges OpenAI and Anthropic at Open Weights
cards.why_it_matters: This is the most significant open-weights frontier model release since DeepSeek R1 — the weights drop July 27th and every researcher needs to know where the open-weights frontier now sits.
cards.summary: Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 is a 2.8T parameter MoE model ranking #2 on Vals AI index, #3 on Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (behind only Claude Fable and GPT-5.6 Sol), and #1 in Frontend Code Arena. The open-to-closed frontier gap is now estimated at 3–5 months, down from 6–9. Weights release scheduled July 27th.

cards.title: TPIPS: Text-Prompted Perceptual Similarity Metric Conditioned on Semantic Aspects
cards.why_it_matters: Current perceptual metrics collapse multi-dimensional similarity into a scalar — this work directly addresses that limitation with a fine-tuned VLM metric useful for evaluating generative models more precisely.
cards.summary: Existing perceptual similarity metrics output a single scalar, ignoring context-specific dimensions like shape vs. color. The authors build a large-scale dataset of human triplet judgments annotated across free-form semantic aspects, then fine-tune a VLM into TPIPS. It outperforms frontier VLMs on human alignment and enables text-guided retrieval, compositional search, and fine-grained generative model evaluation.

cards.title: Patch Policy: Dense ViT Patch Tokens Boost Robot Control with 0.7% of VLA Parameters
cards.why_it_matters: Achieving 40% improvement over global-pooled policies and 18% over fine-tuned OpenVLA-OFT at a fraction of the parameter count is a strong efficiency result for anyone building or evaluating robot learning systems.
cards.summary: Patch Policy introduces a block-causal attention mask that lets transformer-based policies consume dense pre-trained ViT patch tokens without a full VLM backbone. Evaluated across four simulated and three real-world suites, it achieves a 40% relative improvement over state-of-the-art global-pooled baselines and surpasses fine-tuned OpenVLA-OFT by 18% using roughly 0.7% of its parameters.

cards.title: Linguistic Framing of User Beliefs Systematically Shifts LLM Context vs. Prior Knowledge Balance
cards.why_it_matters: Understanding how linguistic form — not just content — controls whether a model follows context or prior knowledge has direct implications for prompt engineering robustness and model evaluation.
cards.summary: A typology of 17 expression-of-belief types spanning form, evidentiality, epistemic stance, and tone was used to benchmark 16 LLMs. Larger and instruction-tuned models tend to be less context-following than smaller or base models. Specific linguistic framings statistically significantly persuade models more than others, revealing exploitable systematic biases independent of factual content.

cards.title: No Universal LLM Discovery Harness: OpenEvolve-Style Search Often Loses to Simpler Alternatives
cards.why_it_matters: Anyone using or designing autonomous LLM-driven discovery or code-search systems needs to know that fixed harness choices behave like untuned hyperparameters, not universal recipes.
cards.summary: Across 30 budget-matched harnesses evaluated on 12 model-problem pairs using over 3.1 million LLM rollouts, no single harness reliably dominates. OpenEvolve-style variants generally underperform simpler alternatives. Early discovery progress predicts final performance; an adaptive allocation strategy that prunes weak runs and reallocates compute outperforms both fixed and non-adaptive ensemble baselines.

cards.title: Simple Training Recipe Beats PIXAR by 26% on Pixel-Level AI Image Tampering Detection OOD
cards.why_it_matters: As VLM-generated image manipulation becomes trivially accessible, robust detection methods that generalize across unseen generation models matter for anyone building content integrity or evaluation pipelines.
cards.summary: The proposed framework uses two strategies: balanced minibatch sampling to prevent optimization bias toward artifacts or clean priors, and late-injection of small amounts of new-domain data after stable convergence. Evaluated on GPT-Images-2.0, Gemini-3.1, FLUX.2, and Seedream 4.5, it outperforms prior SOTA PIXAR by 26.1% relative in average gIoU and 26.8% in cIoU across OOD VLM distributions.

cards.title: Learned Soft Prefixes Override Correct Syllogistic Reasoning in LLMs with Up to 90% Flip Rate
cards.why_it_matters: Showing that opaque continuous-vector prefixes can redirect correct logical judgments across unseen prompt variations exposes a fundamental fragility in reasoning stability relevant to anyone working on LLM robustness or interpretability.
cards.summary: Soft prefixes — opaque continuous vectors prepended at inference — were optimized to flip correct syllogistic answers on Qwen3.6-35B MoE, Qwen3-8B, and Gemma 4 31B. Flip rates reached 72–90% on Qwen3.6 MoE across wording and prompt changes; Gemma validity prefixes retained 54–56% flip rates vs. under 1% for random controls. The dominant effect is a broad answer-preference shift, not symbolic forcing.
